hero.subtitle: Farmers Fightback began defending generational farmers against Government overreach. As a national campaign, we now defend all farming families being bulldozed by big Government and foreign corporations — and we don't back down.

intro.heading: A farmer-led fight for every Aussie farmer.
intro.lede: What started in Wimmera, Victoria is now a national campaign with 35,000+ Australians standing with us. Here's the story in a minute:

latestVideo.lede: Filmed at dawn during lambing. Maiden ewes scattered across the paddock. A contractor reads from a script, threatens a half-million-dollar fine, and demands access the landholder never agreed to.

summary.heading: Stop them devastating Aussie farmers & our national food bowl.
summary.mapAlt: Satellite map of Australia overlaid with thousands of proposed and approved renewable energy and transmission projects
paragraphsHtml: 1,200 kilometres of new high-voltage transmission corridor are now being driven through Australia's best cropping and grazing country. Farmers were never meaningfully consulted. Alternative routes were never seriously considered.

Behind them sit AEMO, the state-owned transmission utilities, and a cast of foreign multinationals — Spain's Iberdrola, Acciona and ACS Group; Germany's RWE and Hochtief; Denmark's Ørsted and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners — now sending contractors onto family farms, unannounced, with threats and clipboards.
We're Farmers Fightback — a farmer-led coalition from regional Australia. We're standing against industrial renewables being forced onto Australia's best farmland. We won't be walked over.

petition.heading: Add your name. Draw the line.
petition.lede: To our Australian and State leaders, we stand with Aussie farmers and will fight any threat to our food security and farming rights.
petition.thanksLede: Welcome to the fight, {{first}}. We'll email you when the next video drops and when farmers need backup at the gate.
petition.shareText: I just signed the Farmers Fightback petition. Join 70,000+ Australians: farmersfightback.com

quote.name: — Rob, fourth-generation farmer

items.headline: VicGrid: Marnoo farmer blockade halts 15 property surveys
Farmers challenge VNI West power project access in Victoria
Farmers dig in: new bill 'acquisition by stealth'
National broadcast leads to 5,000 new supporters in a single night

takeAction.heading: Pick a fight. Stand up.
takeAction.lede: Three live campaigns. Each one needs your name. Sign one, sign them all — every signature is a letter to Spring Street and Canberra.

campaigns.kicker: Live · Remove us from the REZ
campaigns.title: Remove us from the REZ.
campaigns.summary: Western Victorian farmers said NO at every consultation. VicGrid ignored them. Demand the district be removed from the Grampians Wimmera REZ.

campaigns.title: Hold the Gate.
campaigns.summary: Stand with farming families when corporate contractors come for their property. 100% failure rate so far. Let's keep it that way.

campaigns.title: Resign, Minister.
campaigns.summary: Greg called 000 on trespassers. Vic Police charged the farmer. On 27 April 2026 the DPP withdrew every charge. There was no case. The Minister must resign.

campaigns.title: End the fuel & fertiliser crisis.
campaigns.summary: Input costs up 200% in a year. Crop prices flat. Farmers being asked to plant at a loss. Demand a freeze, an inquiry, and a path back to viable.

remove-us-from-the-rez.subheading: The farmers of western Victoria have said NO — again and again. It's time VicGrid listened.
context: The Victorian Government wants to force a Renewable Energy Zone onto some of the most productive farmland in the state — despite the landowners saying NO at every single consultation.

Farmers Fightback represents the farming families on the corridor who have mapped their properties to show unanimous opposition. They don't want turbines. They don't want solar farms. They don't want battery storage. They don't want transmission lines. None of it.
VicGrid has ignored them every time. This petition demands they stop.
petitionStatement: The affected farming district be immediately reclassified as an "Avoidance Area" or "Deprioritised Area" under the Victorian Transmission Plan.
The district be completely removed from the Grampians Wimmera Renewable Energy Zone.
VicGrid comply with its obligations under the International Association of Public Participation (IAP2) framework and genuinely respect community consultation outcomes.
No renewable energy infrastructure — including turbines, solar panels, battery energy storage systems (BESS), or transmission lines — be approved on land where the landowner has explicitly refused.

trustBadges.label: Farmer Led
trustBadges.sub: Built by the people it affects.

remove-us-from-the-rez.thanksLede: You're signature {{count}} on the Remove Us From The REZ petition. Share it with everyone you know — VicGrid is far harder to ignore at 25,000 voices than at 5,000.
remove-us-from-the-rez.shareText: Victorian farmers said NO to being forced into a Renewable Energy Zone. VicGrid ignored them. Sign the petition at farmersfightback.com #FarmersFightback #RemoveUsFromTheREZ

whyMatters.heading: They said NO. VicGrid didn't listen.
whyMatters.body: The farming families on the corridor have engaged in good faith with VicGrid at every consultation opportunity. They attended meetings. They made submissions. They mapped every single property to show unified opposition. The result? They were placed into Tier 1 and Tier 2 areas, and then shoved into the Grampians Wimmera REZ anyway. This isn't consultation. It's a tick-box exercise. Farmers Fightback has lodged a formal complaint that VicGrid has violated the International Association of Public Participation (IAP2) standards it is legally bound to follow.

whyMatters.heading: This is some of the most productive farmland in Australia.
whyMatters.body: The land targeted for the REZ is highly productive food and fibre country. These aren't hobby farms. These are generational agricultural businesses that feed the nation. Industrial renewable infrastructure — turbines, solar arrays, battery storage, transmission lines — would permanently damage this land and destroy its productive capacity. You cannot run a precision farming operation under a transmission line. You cannot lamb under turbines. You cannot insure a property next to a battery storage facility. The damage is not temporary. It is permanent.

whyMatters.heading: The environment they claim to protect will be destroyed.
whyMatters.body: The district contains internationally recognised Key Biodiversity Areas with some of the most critically threatened woodland and grassland ecosystems in Victoria. These areas provide habitat for critically endangered birds and fauna. The farming properties themselves provide the biolinks — the ecological corridors — between these irreplaceable habitats. The Victorian Government's own biodiversity mapping shows this. Their own handbook for renewable energy development says areas like this should be avoided. They're ignoring their own rules.

whyMatters.heading: The CFA won't fight fires on REZ land.
whyMatters.body: Local Country Fire Authority brigades have made it clear: they will not fight fires on any property hosting renewable energy infrastructure. These brigades are active members of FAROV (Fire and Rescue Organisations of Victoria) and they are not bluffing. In a region where bushfire is a constant threat, this alone makes the area completely unsuitable for a Renewable Energy Zone. People could die.

whyMatters.heading: Unprecedented powers, zero accountability.
whyMatters.body: The recently announced VicGrid Stage Reform Bill gives VicGrid extraordinary and unprecedented powers to operate within a REZ — with virtually no accountability. These powers will have immediate impact on land valuations and liveability. The Victorian Transmission Plan also assumes VNI West will proceed before it has even gone through the formal planning approval process. The government is treating the outcome as a fait accompli. If the planning process isn't independent, what is the point of having one?

whyMatters.heading: We are not misinformed. We do not consent.
whyMatters.body: The community wants to make something crystal clear: they are not misinformed. They are not confused. They are not being manipulated by outside interests. They are successful farmers and successful business owners who perfectly understand what is being proposed — and they do not consent. No amount of "community benefit funds" will change this. The community calls these payments what they are: bribes. Blood money designed to buy silence while their land is industrialised by foreign-owned corporations that will never set foot on the soil they destroy.

bottomCta.heading: Stand with the farmers.
bottomCta.body: Sign the petition. Share it with everyone you know. These families have done everything right — and they've been ignored. Help us make sure that stops today.

hold-the-gate.heroEyebrow: Join 20,000 Australians standing up for our farmers
hold-the-gate.headingHtml: Help our farmers, before it's too late.
context: Australian agriculture is the envy of the world, contributing over $80 billion to our national economy and sustaining rural communities that have stewarded the land for generations. In Victoria alone, our farmers produce $20.1 billion in food and fibre exports every year—more than any other state. But this security is being dismantled.

We are currently facing the largest forced acquisition of agricultural land in our history. Projects like VNI West plan to tear through the heart of our food bowl with 400km of high-voltage lines supported by steel towers up to 80 metres high—the height of a 25-story building.
This is not just an eyesore; it is a disaster waiting to happen.
hold-the-gate.petitionDeclarationLine1: TO OUR AUSTRALIAN AND STATE LEADERS…
hold-the-gate.petitionDeclaration: WE STAND WITH AUSSIE FARMERS, AND WILL FIGHT AGAINST ANY THREAT TO OUR FOOD SECURITY AND FARMING RIGHTS.

trustBadges.label: Farmer-led
trustBadges.sub: Built by the families it affects.

trustBadges.label: Authorised
trustBadges.sub: Authorised by Ben Duxson, Wallaloo VIC.

hold-the-gate.thanksLede: You're signature {{count}}. Share the petition with everyone you know — every name we add makes Spring St harder to ignore.
hold-the-gate.shareText: Help our farmers fightback, before it's too late. Sign the petition → farmersfightback.com #FarmersFightback

whyMatters.heading: $80 billion of food on the line.
whyMatters.body: Australian agriculture contributes over $80 billion to our national economy. Victoria's share alone is $20.1 billion. Industrial transmission lines through prime farmland don't just take a slice of one farm — they destroy the productivity of every paddock they cross.
whyMatters.imageAlt: Drone shot of a rally of farmers and supporters

whyMatters.heading: Fire crews won't fly near transmission lines.
whyMatters.body: Local CFA brigades have said it plainly: firefighting aircraft cannot operate safely near high-voltage transmission lines. Ground crews maintain a 25-metre no-go zone. In bushfire country, this isn't a hypothetical — it's the difference between containment and catastrophe.
whyMatters.imageAlt: Transmission tower burning in a wheat field

whyMatters.heading: Heavy machinery brings weeds, disease, and ruined paddocks.
whyMatters.body: Forced contractor access drags weeds, soil pathogens, and biosecurity risks onto farms that have spent decades being managed carefully. Once fragmented by easements, modern precision farming becomes impossible — the maths stops working.
whyMatters.imageAlt: Biosecurity warning sign on a farm gate

whyMatters.heading: Cost-blown to $11.4 billion. Still climbing.
whyMatters.body: VNI West was originally costed at $3.8 billion. It's now $11.4 billion. The Government keeps signing the cheque using farmers' land as collateral. Every blowout makes the case for an alternate route stronger — but they keep refusing to consider one.
whyMatters.imageAlt: Children with hand-made 'Stop the Towers' signs on the Parliament steps

bottomCta.heading: Stand with Aussie farmers.
bottomCta.body: Sign the petition. Share it with everyone you know. These farms feed the nation — and they're being taken without consent. Help us make sure the Government can't ignore us.

baldwins.heading: Charges dropped. The Minister must resign.
baldwins.lede: Greg Baldwin rang triple zero to report trespassers on his own farm. Vic Police charged the farmer. On 27 April 2026, the Director of Public Prosecutions withdrew every charge. Greg does not want sympathy. Greg wants the Victorian Energy Minister to resign.

demands.text: Immediate suspension of all contractor access attempts on the Baldwins' property pending an independent investigation.
A public apology from VicGrid for the conduct of contractors who threatened the Baldwins with $6,000 fines on their own land.
Statutory protection of all soldier-settlement properties from compulsory access without proper notice and consent.
Full disclosure of every contractor script, identification protocol, and access plan VicGrid is using on Victorian farms.

baldwins.thanksLede: You just became signature {{count}} on the Baldwins petition. The bigger this gets, the harder Spring Street will find it to ignore.
baldwins.shareText: Charges dropped. The DPP withdrew every charge against Greg Baldwin. The Victorian Energy Minister must resign. Sign the petition at farmersfightback.com #ResignMinister #ChargesDropped

fuel-and-fertiliser.heading: Plant at a loss, or don't plant at all.
fuel-and-fertiliser.lede: Fuel and fertiliser costs have risen 200% in a single year. Crop prices are flat. The maths stopped working. Farming families are being asked to plant a season at a guaranteed loss — or walk away from the only thing they know. We're demanding a freeze, an inquiry, and a path back to viable.

demands.text: An immediate ACCC inquiry into fuel and fertiliser pricing and supply concentration in Australian agriculture.
A 12-month rebate scheme on diesel and key fertilisers for primary producers operating below cost.
Mandatory contract transparency between fertiliser distributors and Australian farmers — no more take-it-or-leave-it.
A national strategic-reserve plan for fertiliser inputs to insulate Australian food production from offshore price shocks.

fuel-and-fertiliser.thanksLede: You're signature {{count}} on the Fuel & Fertiliser petition. We'll keep you updated as this campaign moves through the inquiry process.
fuel-and-fertiliser.shareText: Australian farmers are being asked to plant at a loss while fuel and fertiliser costs double. Sign the petition for an ACCC inquiry and a rebate scheme at farmersfightback.com

theFight.heading: What we're fighting
theFight.lede: Farmers Fightback is a national, farmer-led movement standing against the persecution of farmers on all fronts - and standing with the families fighting to keep their homes and their way of life.

panels.title: Australian food security.
panels.body: Australian agriculture contributes over $80 billion to our economy. Industrial energy infrastructure is tearing up this country and will permanently damage the most productive farmland in the southern hemisphere. We're fighting to keep food on Australian tables and stop the decimation of our farming industry.
panels.imageAlt: Combine harvester working a wheat paddock at harvest time

panels.title: Property rights.
panels.body: Your gate is your gate. The right to refuse access to your own property is the most basic test of whether the law still applies to the Government. Greg Baldwin was slapped with empty charges when calling 000 on a trespasser. Farmers should not end up in handcuffs for maintaining their fundamental property rights.
panels.imageAlt: Aerial drone view across cropping country and paddocks

panels.title: The forced takeover of family farms.
panels.body: Across Australia big-corporate projects like VNI West are burdening communities with thousands of kilometres of high-voltage line. Tearing through the heart of Australia's most productive agricultural food bowl. We must stop 80-metre steel towers, 70-metre easements, forced acquisition of farms. The same Government that promised consultation is now using forced-access powers to override every NO. We're fighting that, every farm, every gate.
panels.imageAlt: Aerial view of a VNI West transmission tower construction site on farmland

panels.title: The criminalisation of peaceful resistance.
panels.body: Greg Baldwin is an example of just one farmer who was wrongly charged. When he found a Government contractor trespassing on his farm, he called triple 000. He was the one charged with a crime, not the trespasser. This is just one of hundreds of examples of wrongly persecution of farmers, and our leaders must heed the call or resign.
panels.imageAlt: A police officer speaking with a group of farmers gathered under a gum tree

theFight.ctaBody: Four live campaigns. Sign one. Sign them all. The Government has billions. We have you.

contact.lede: Send us a message and we'll come back to you. Whether you're a farmer at the gate, a journalist on deadline, a volunteer ready to turn up, or someone who just wants to help — write to us using the form below.

form.heading: Send us a message
form.doneBody: Thanks for reaching out. We'll be in touch within 48 hours.

donorPage.heading: They have billions. We have you.
donorPage.lede: Farmers Fightback is funded by Australians, not corporations. Every dollar is spent on the things that move the needle — ads that put the story in front of more people, video production, organising on the ground, and the legal fight to repeal forced-access powers.

amounts.tag: A camera kit for a farmer
A regional billboard for a week
Fund a full TV campaign run

achievements.heading: What your dollars have already paid for.
achievements.imageAlt: Farmers and supporters at a Farmers Fightback rally holding cardboard masks of state politicians
bullets: Mobilised 54,000+ Australians to stand with farming families against forced acquisition of farmland.
Held the Victorian Government at the gate — dozens of farming families refused unauthorised access and not one was forced through during Hold the Gate.

Exposed the embarrassment of a prosecution that never had a case — the DPP withdrew every charge against Greg Baldwin. The Government criminalised a farmer for calling triple zero on a trespasser, and lost.
Dragged forced-access powers into the light — provisions the Government buried in energy legislation are now public knowledge and under cross-party scrutiny.
Built a national campaign from scratch in five months — website, petitions, donations, email, volunteers. No lobbyists. No parties. Just farmers.
achievements.kicker: They have billions. We have you.

donorPage.monthlyHeading: Or give monthly — they're not going anywhere, we're not either.
donorPage.fineprint: Stripe-secured · All amounts in AUD · Not tax-deductible.

volunteer.heading: Volunteer with us.
volunteer.lede: Your time and skills can change the course of this fight. Whether you can give two hours a week or twenty, there's a place for you.
volunteer.rolesIntro: Every role matters. Pick what fits your life right now.

roles.title: Hold the Gate volunteer
roles.body: Be on the alert network. When contractors arrive at a farm gate, we ping volunteers nearby and you turn up to witness, film, and stand peacefully with the family.

roles.title: Local organiser
roles.body: Run your district — meetings, doorknocks, letterbox drops, signups at the markets. Help build the local chapter that holds your MPs to account.

roles.title: Digital campaigning
roles.body: Help us reach more Australians online — paid social, organic content, email lists, supporter onboarding. Tech-comfortable, no specific platform required.

roles.title: Content & comms
roles.body: Write, edit, design, film, cut. Turn the campaign's raw material into the next viral short, op-ed, or supporter dispatch.

roles.title: Events
roles.body: Public meetings, tractor rallies, hall-hires, the Spring St handover. If you've organised a school fete or a wedding, you can organise this.

roles.title: Legal & policy support
roles.body: Submissions, FOIs, briefing notes, parliamentary inquiry tracking. Lawyers, academics, and ex-public-servants especially welcome.

volunteer.doneBody: Thanks for stepping up. We'll be in touch within 48 hours with your next move.
volunteer.altBody: Every contribution matters — your signature, your dollar, your time. Each one moves the needle.

mediaPage.heading: Media & press enquiries.
mediaPage.lede: Same-day reply during business hours. We can provide on-record statements, interview subjects, photography, b-roll, and access to families directly affected by VNI West and forced-access powers.

primaryEmail.label: Media outreach
primaryEmail.blurb: Press enquiries, interview requests, statements, asset packs.

aboutUs.heading: Left with no choice. Farmers Fightback.
aboutUs.subheading: Born for the soil. Built for the fight.

stat.label: Australians standing up for our farmers

sections.title: From a kitchen-table revolt to a national front.
paragraphs: This campaign was born from the soil of the Wimmera, sparked by a small but determined group of farming families who refused to stand by as their livelihoods were mapped over by bureaucrats in city offices.

What began as a local stand against the VNI West "Option 5" route has grown into a fierce defence of our region's future. We saw firsthand how the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) and government bodies treated our prime agricultural land — generations of stewardship and high-quality protein production — as merely vacant space for infrastructure.

Our community faced the terrifying reality of high-voltage transmission lines cutting through our paddocks, bringing with them unacceptable biosecurity risks, dismantling our ability to use modern ag-tech, and — most critically — creating a fire hazard that would leave our volunteer brigades unable to defend our homes. We realised that if we didn't hold the line here, no farming community in Australia would be safe.

sections.title: A united, national front for farmers' rights.
paragraphs: Today, Farmers Fightback is a national organisation uniting farmers, food producers, and rural families across Australia to preserve autonomy over our land. We are fighting back against not only reckless energy infrastructure like VNI West, but also the wave of ridiculous and punitive legislation that threatens the viability of our industry.

We fundamentally reject a system that steamrolls rural communities and sacrifices the very land that feeds the nation. From blocking access to Transmission Company Victoria (TCV) to challenging heavy-handed regulatory overreach, we are demanding a fair go.
We stand for the right to farm without interference — ensuring that the voice of the bush is powerful enough to stop bad laws and protect intergenerational farms for the future.

items.title: Right to farm
items.body: Without interference. Without forced access. Without being mapped over by people who've never set foot on the soil.

items.title: Food security first
items.body: Australia's prime agricultural land is not vacant space for infrastructure. It is the food bowl of the nation. We defend it.

items.title: Real consultation
items.body: Genuine community consent — not a tick-box exercise after the route is already drawn. Communities must have meaningful power to say no.

items.title: Intergenerational
items.body: Family farms have been stewarded for generations. We fight to keep them in the hands of the families who built them — for the generations that come next.

cta.heading: Stand with the farmers.
cta.body: We've gone from a kitchen-table conversation to a national movement of 54,000+ Australians. The next step is yours.

aboutUs.authorised: Authorised by Ben Duxson, Farmers Fightback
